examples: Severe nuclear damage indicated by pyknosis and karyolysis are customarily taken as evidence of cell necrosis.
Results the Nissl bodies in the spinal cord and the DRG neurons were observed to have pyknosis, accompanied by collapse and karyolysis of Nissl body.
Many nuclei have become pyknotic (shrunken and dark) and have then undergone karorrhexis (fragmentation) and karyolysis (dissolution). The cytoplasm and cell borders are not recognizable.
